---
title: "![](logoUMAG.jpg){width=4cm}"
output:
  pdf_document:
    includes:
      before_body: titulo.sty
    keep_tex: yes
    number_sections: no
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
bibliography: SA_krill.bib
csl: apa.csl
link-citations: yes
linkcolor: blue
indent: no
header-includes:
- \usepackage{fancyhdr}
- \pagestyle{fancy}
- \fancyhf{}
- \lfoot[\thepage]{}
- \rfoot[]{\thepage}
---

```{=tex}
\fontsize{10}{18}
\selectfont
```

```{r setup_v2, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE, message=F}
rm(list = ls())
options(bitmapType = "cairo")
```

```{r setup2, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
library(knitr)
opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "  ",
  fig.align = 'center',
  cache = FALSE,
  tidy.opts = list(width.cutoff = 55),
  tidy = TRUE
)
```

```{r config_global, echo=FALSE}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOQUE DE CONFIGURACIÓN GLOBAL — v2
# Cambiar aquí para ajustar todos los parámetros del MSE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# v2: centralizado aquí en lugar de disperso en el documento

CFG <- list(
  ss3_dir    = "s1.4",            # directorio SS3
  run_name   = "krill.stock.wAP", # nombre del run SS3
  n_iter     = 100,               # iteraciones MSE (usar 500-1000 en producción)
  proj_years = 30,                # horizonte proyección (v2: 30 años, antes era 10)
  seed       = 123,               # semilla reproducibilidad
  tac_cc145  = 145000,            # TAC fijo actual CCAMLR subárea 48.1 (t)
  tac_cc620  = 620000,            # TAC fijo propuesto área 48 (t)
  data_lag   = 1,
  mgmt_lag   = 1,
  frq        = 1,
  # Fracciones de B0 para métricas CCAMLR
  ccamlr_target = 0.75,           # P(B > 0.75 B0) — objetivo escapatoria
  ccamlr_limit  = 0.20            # P(B > 0.20 B0) — límite crítico
)
```

\pagebreak

# INTRODUCTION

This work presents a Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE) framework applied to Antarctic krill (*Euphausia superba*) in the Western Antarctic Peninsula (WAP), representing the first implementation of an MSE based on an integrated stock assessment model using `SS3` (Stock Synthesis 3) as the operating model for this species.

The simulation framework is structured following the general principles developed under the ICES MSE BlackSteam Working Group, and it draws heavily on methodologies established in the **FLR** (Fisheries Library in R) framework. The MSE is built upon FLR packages such as `FLCore`, `FLAssess`, and `FLasher`, which provide the computational tools and object-oriented architecture to implement and evaluate management strategies in a reproducible and flexible way.

The objective of this MSE is to test the performance of alternative Harvest Control Rules (HCRs) in meeting predefined conservation and yield objectives under uncertainty in stock dynamics and observation processes. The operating model (OM) represents the "true" krill population dynamics, incorporating recruitment variability, environmental forcing, and stochasticity in both biological and fishery processes. The estimation model (EM) simulates the application of a stock assessment under realistic monitoring and assessment error, informing annual catch limits.

By adopting this approach, the study provides a transparent and policy-relevant evaluation of candidate HCRs for krill fisheries in the WAP, in this case, the currently catch limit. This research is developed within an academic framework and aims to provide a methodological contribution to the ongoing discussions regarding the revision of krill new management strategy under CCAMLR. By demonstrating the feasibility and utility of implementing a Management Strategy Evaluation using an SS3-based operating model and tools from the FLR framework, this study offers a scientifically robust foundation to support the development of adaptive, ecosystem-informed harvest strategies aligned with the precautionary and ecosystem-based principles of CCAMLR.

All model code process and simulation tools will made openly available to the scientific community, encouraging transparency, reproducibility, and constructive feedback to refine and strengthen this approach through collaborative development.

```{r libs, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(FLCore)
library(ggplotFL)
library(mse)
library(FLRef)
library(ggpubr)
library(mseviz)
library(r4ss)
library(doParallel)
library(here)
library(ss3sim)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)   # v2: para formateo de ejes CCAMLR
```

## Short-Cut MSE for Harvest Control Rule (HCR) in krill

Different to Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE) simulation design (Punt et al. 2017), the MSE `shortcut` approach omits the step of the annual updating of the estimation model (assessment) in the feedback control. Instead, it emulates an annual update of the benchmark assessment model by passing outcomes ($SSB$ and $F$ or equivalents) from the 'true' age-structured dynamics of the operating model (OM), with assessment error, to the harvest control rule (HCR) and catch implementation system.

The HCRs are implemented using a simulated feedback control loop between the implementation system and the operating model, where the implementation system translates the emulated assessment outcome into the Total Allowable Catch (TAC) advice. The feedback control loop accounts for the lag between the last year of data used in the assessment and the implementation year of catch advice ($C_{adv}$).

For Antarctic krill in the Western Antarctic Peninsula (WAP), the implementation system of the harvest control rule is based on the assumption that advice is given for year $y+1$ based on an assessment completed in year $y$, using data available up to year $y-1$. The implementation of the derived $C_{adv}$ through the HCR therefore requires projection of stock dynamics via a short-term forecast. To do this, numbers-at-size (or -age) are projected through the year of assessment. Parameters such as recruitment, natural mortality ($M$), weight-at-size, and maturity are set as the mean of the last three years. A projection based on a fixed exploitation rate or fishing mortality corresponding to the last year ($y-1$) is used through to the implementation year ($y+1$).

The goal of this application is to test the robustness of the current harvest control rule used by CCAMLR for krill, which consists of a fixed TAC of 145,000 tonnes per year for subarea 48.1 (or the proposed 620,000 t for the full Area 48). The shortcut MSE approach facilitates this by reducing computational complexity and avoiding the need for annual full assessments, while still capturing feedback effects between advice and stock dynamics. Here, the MSE short-cut approach is implemented using the tools available in the Fisheries Library for R (FLR; Kell et al., 2007; [FLR Library](https://flr-project.org/)

## Software Implementation

This analysis was conducted using R version `r R.version$major`.`r R.version$minor` [@R-base] with the following key packages:

1. Core MSE Framework:
   - FLR ecosystem [@FLCore; @FLBRP]
   - `mse` package [@mse-pkg] for management procedure evaluation

2. Stock Assessment:
   - SS3 integration via `r4ss` [@r4ss]
   - Simulation testing with `ss3sim` [@ss3sim]

3. Visualization & Reporting:
   - `ggplot2` [@ggplot2] for graphics
   - `kableExtra` [@kableExtra] for publication-ready tables

```{r package-versions, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
pkgs <- c("FLCore", "mse", "FLBRP", "r4ss", "ss3sim", "ggplot2", "kableExtra")
vers <- sapply(pkgs, function(x) as.character(packageVersion(x)))

knitr::kable(
  data.frame(Package = pkgs, Version = vers),
  caption = "Key package versions used in analysis",
  booktabs = TRUE
) %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(latex_options = "hold_position")
```

## Glossary

The following glossary summarizes key HCR parameters and associated target and limit reference points
that are considered for tuning the candidate HCRs to optimise the trade-offs between maximising fishing
opportunity and risk:

+ $F_{MSY}$: target reference point for fishing mortality at Fmsy (or its proxy)

+ $B_{MSY}$: the average biomass around which the biomass fluctuated when fishing at $F_{MSY}$ or its proxy

+ $B_{lim}$: a deterministic biomass limit reference point below which a stock is considered to have reduced reproductive capacity. Here $B_{lim}$ was set to $0.20B_0$ (CCAMLR convention)

+ $B_{pa}$: a precautionary biomass reference point set with high probability that biomass is above $B_{lim}$

+ $C_{adv}$: advised catch as output of the management procedure

+ $B_{trigger}$: biomass trigger point of the HCR, below which fishing mortality is reduced relative to $F_{tgt}$

+ **CCAMLR Metrics** (v2 addition):
  - $P(B > 0.75 B_0)$: probability SSB exceeds 75% of virgin biomass (escapement objective)
  - $P(B > 0.20 B_0)$: probability SSB remains above the collapse threshold

\pagebreak

# METHODOLOGY

## Operating Model developing

Build `FLStock` object.

```{r read_ss3, eval=TRUE}
# v2: usar CFG para rutas
dir1 <- here(CFG$ss3_dir)
run  <- CFG$run_name
stk  <- window(ss3om::readFLSss3(dir1))
stk  <- simplify(stk)
stk@m.spwn[]       <- 0
stk@harvest.spwn[] <- 0
sr      <- ss3om::readFLSRss3(dir1, run)
stk@name <- run
stk@desc <- "2024, CCAMLR, SS3"
out     <- ss3om::readOutputss3(dir1)
# save(stk, sr, out, file = "R_Data/base.model1.4.RData")
```

```{r load_om, eval=FALSE}
run_name <- "base.model1.4"
load(file = file.path("R_Data", paste0(run_name, ".RData")), verbose = TRUE)
```

```{r plot_stk}
stka <- stk
plot(stk)
```

## Retune Stock-Recruitment

```{r sr_retuning}
# Extraer parámetros SR desde SS3
s  <- params(sr)[[1]]
R0 <- params(sr)[[2]]
B0 <- params(sr)[[3]]

# v2: extraer B0 directamente de derived_quants para consistencia
B0_ss3 <- out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == "SSB_Virgin"]
cat("B0 SS3 (derived_quants):", round(B0_ss3), "t\n")
cat("B0 SR params:           ", round(B0), "t\n")
B0 <- B0_ss3  # v2: usar siempre el B0 del modelo, no del SR

sr1 <- srrTMB(as.FLSR(stka, model = bevholtSV),
              spr0   = mean(spr0y(stk)),
              r0.pr  = c(R0, 0.0001),
              s      = s,
              s.est  = FALSE)

sr2 <- as.FLSR(stk, model = bevholt)
sr3 <- fmle(sr2)
```

```{r sr_plot, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, fig.cap="Comparison of SR function from SS3 and retuned single-sex with FLSRTMB"}
plotsrs(FLSRs(ss3 = sr, sr = sr1))
plot(sr3)
```

Check that the `fbar` range is consistent with `ss.starter` input.

```{r}
range(stk)
```

```{r mainvar, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=6, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Seasonal stock trajectories"}
plot(stk,
     metrics = list(
       SSB   = function(x) unitSums(ssb(x)[,,,1]),
       F     = function(x) fbar(x),
       Catch = function(x) catch(x),
       Rec   = function(x) unitSums(rec(x))
     )) +
  theme_bw() +
  ylab("F") + xlab("Year") +
  facet_wrap(~qname, scales = "free_y")
```

## Plot SS3 Stock Dynamics

```{r trayec, fig.height=5, fig.width=7, fig.cap="Stock assessment trajectories at age"}
plotdyn(stk) + theme_bw() + scale_color_viridis_d(option = "E")
```

\newpage

```{r trayec2, fig.height=5, fig.width=7, fig.cap="Stock biology trajectories at age"}
plotbioyr(stk) +
  ggtitle(paste0(stk@name)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "E")
```

\newpage

```{r trayec3, fig.height=6, fig.cap="Annual stock quantities at age"}
plotbioage(stk) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "E")
```

\pagebreak

## Consistency checks using backtesting

```{r seed_set}
set.seed(CFG$seed)
```

Get bias adjusted recruitment deviations from ss3 model.

```{r simplify_stk}
if (dims(stk)$season > 1) {
  stka <- simplify(stk, 'season', weighted = TRUE, harvest = TRUE)
  discards.wt(stka) <- stock.wt(stka)
  stka@discards     <- computeDiscards(stka)
  sra <- sr
  params(sra) <- FLPar(an(sr@params[, 1]),
                        params = rownames(sr@params))
} else {
  sra  <- sr1
  stka <- stk
}
```

```{r rec_devs}
yrs     <- an(dimnames(stk)$year)
recruit <- out$recruit[out$recruit$Yr %in% yrs, ]
dms     <- list(year = yrs)

sigR <- mean(an(out$sigma_R_info[1:2, "SD_of_devs_over_sigma_R"]))

residuals <- FLQuant(
  exp(recruit$dev - 0.5 * recruit$biasadjuster * sigR^2),
  dimnames = c(age = 0, dms),
  units = ""
)

recs <- FLQuant(recruit$pred_recr,
                dimnames = c(age = 0, dms),
                units = "")

if (dims(stk)$unit == 2) recs <- expand(recs, unit = c("F", "M"))
```

```{r backtest}
if (dims(stka)$unit == 3) {
  yrs <- an(dimnames(stka)$year)
}
testC <- fwd(stka, sr = recs[, ac(yrs[-1])],
             control = fwdControl(
               year  = yrs[-1],
               value = (unitSums(catch(stka)[, ac(yrs[-1])])),
               quant = "catch"))

testF <- fwd(stka, sr = recs[, ac(yrs[-1])],
             control = fwdControl(
               year  = yrs[-1],
               value = unitMeans(fbar(stka)[, ac(yrs[-1])]),
               quant = "fbar"))
```

```{r hc1, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=9, fig.height=7,fig.cap="Comparison of stock trajectories from ss3om and backtest"}
plot(window(FLStocks(ss3om = stka, backtestC = testC, backtestF = testF))) +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~qname, scale = "free_y")
```

## Estimate candidate reference points

```{r refpts_calc}
# Recruitment deviations and autocorrelation
recyrs   <- recruit$Yr[recruit$era == "Main"]

# v2: FIX — factor *2 solo si el modelo es sex-structured (2 unidades)
# En v1 se multiplicaba siempre por 2, lo cual sobreestimaba los devs en modelos single-sex
n_units  <- dims(stk)$unit
maindevs <- unitSums(residuals[, ac(recyrs)]) * n_units
rho      <- cor(maindevs[, -1], maindevs[, -length(maindevs)])
sigmaR   <- out$sigma_R_in

cat("rho:", round(rho, 3), "| sigmaR:", round(sigmaR, 3), "\n")

# MSY reference points desde SS3
Bmsy <- B0 * 0.75  # CCAMLR: objetivo = 75% B0
Fmsy <- out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == "F_1997"]
MSY  <- out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == "Dead_Catch_MSY"]

# Observation errors
ay     <- out$endyr
SSBcv  <- out$derived_quants$StdDev[out$derived_quants$Label == paste0("SSB_", ay)] /
          out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == paste0("SSB_", ay)]
Fcv    <- out$derived_quants$StdDev[out$derived_quants$Label == paste0("F_", ay)] /
          out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == paste0("F_", ay)]
```

```{r refpts_thresholds}
# v2: CCAMLR reference points — 0.20 B0 como límite, 0.75 B0 como objetivo
Blim     <- B0 * CFG$ccamlr_limit   # 0.20 B0
Bpa      <- B0 * CFG$ccamlr_limit   # igual que Blim (por ahora — puede ajustarse)
Btri.eq  <- B0 * CFG$ccamlr_limit   # trigger también en 0.20 B0

Feq      <- out$derived_quants$Value[out$derived_quants$Label == "F_2020"]
Fp05.eq  <- 0.018

cat("Bmsy (0.75 B0):", round(Bmsy), "t\n")
cat("Blim (0.20 B0):", round(Blim), "t\n")
cat("Fmsy (SS3):", round(Fmsy, 4), "\n")
cat("Feq  (F2020):", round(Feq, 4), "\n")
```

> Note that in this case the "true" $F_{MSY}$ from SS3 is smaller than $F_{eq}$. This may be explained by the presence of the harvest control rule resulting in effective fishing, on average, below Btrigger.

```{r fwdB4F_fun}
# v2: nfy aumentado a 100 años para convergencia al equilibrio
# (30 años en v1 era insuficiente para stocks de alta variabilidad como krill)
fwdB4F <- function(stock, sr, Fs = 0.2, nfy = 100) {
  if (class(stock) == "FLStockR") {
    stock <- as(stock, "FLStock")
  }
  fyrs <- (dims(stock)$maxyear + 1):(dims(stock)$maxyear + nfy)
  stkf <- stf(stock, nfy)
  bx   <- do.call(c, lapply(an(Fs), function(x) {
    ictrl <- fwdControl(data.frame(year = fyrs, quant = "fbar", value = x))
    out_  <- fwd(stkf, sr = sr, control = ictrl)
    an(tail(unitSums(ssb(out_))))
  }))
  data.frame(F = an(Fs), B = bx)
}
```

```{r Ftgt_grid}
Fx   <- c(rev(seq(0.1, 1, 0.1)))
Ftgt <- FLPar(c(Fmsy, Fx * Feq),
              params = c("Fmsy.om", paste0(Fx, ".Feq")))
Ftgt
```

```{r Bftgt_calc}
Bftgt <- fwdB4F(stka, sra, Fs = Ftgt, nfy = 100)
Bftgt
```

```{r save_ftune, eval=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
save(Fx, Ftgt, Bftgt, file = "rdata/ftune.sbr.rdata")
```

```{r load_ftune, echo=FALSE}
load(file = "rdata/ftune.sbr.rdata")
```

```{r Ftgt_table}
Ftgt.tune <- FLPar(c(Bftgt$F, Bftgt$B),
                   params = c("Fmsy.om",
                              paste0("Feq", Fx),
                              "Bmsy.om",
                              paste0("Bftgt", Fx)))

np    <- an(nrow(Ftgt.tune))
df    <- data.frame(
  Tune     = rownames(Ftgt.tune)[1:(np/2)],
  Ftgt     = round(an(Ftgt.tune)[1:(np/2)], 3),
  Btrigger = round(Btri.eq, 1),
  Btgt     = round(an(Ftgt.tune)[(np/2+1):np], 1),
  "xB0"    = round(an(Ftgt.tune)[(np/2+1):np] / B0, 3)
)
df$Btrigger[1] <- 0
df$xB0         <- round(df$xB0, 2)
```

```{r}
kbl(df,
    format    = "latex",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    caption   = "Tuning grid: F targets and corresponding equilibrium biomass") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"))
```

```{r refpts_obj}
refpts <- FLPar(
  Fmsy     = Fmsy,
  Feq      = Feq,
  Bmsy     = Ftgt.tune["Bmsy.om"],
  Beq      = Ftgt.tune["Bftgt0.3"],
  Blim     = Blim,
  Bpa      = Bpa,
  Btrigger = Btri.eq,
  B0       = B0,
  R0       = R0
)
refpts
```

## Create `FLStockR` with `@refpts`

```{r stkr_build}
stkr          <- FLStockR(stka)
stkr@refpts   <- refpts
```

```{r spars_build}
spars <- FLPar(
  s      = s,
  sigmaR = sigmaR,
  rho    = rho,
  Fcv    = Fcv,
  SSBcv  = SSBcv
)
```

```{r timedyn, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height=7, fig.width=9, fig.cap="Status Advice plot showing stock trajectories"}
plotAdvice(stkr)
```

```{r save_om_ss3ref, eval=FALSE}
save(stk, stka, stkr, refpts, sr, sra, spars,
     file = "rdata/om.ss3ref.sbr.rdata")
```

## Set up short-cut MSE

Load `OM` conditioned to Stock Synthesis.

```{r load_om2}
load("rdata/om.ss3ref.sbr.rdata", verbose = TRUE)
```

```{r mse_horizon}
# data year
dy  <- dims(stka)$maxyear
# v2: horizonte 30 años en lugar de 10 para capturar dinámica de largo plazo
# 10 años (v1) era insuficiente para evaluar métricas CCAMLR de largo plazo
fy  <- dy + CFG$proj_years
ay  <- dy + 1
iy  <- ay
cat("Data year:", dy, "| Final year:", fy,
    "| Projection:", CFG$proj_years, "años\n")
```

```{r iterations}
it <- CFG$n_iter
cat("Iteraciones:", it, "\n")
```

```{r propagate_stk}
stki <- propagate(stka, it)
```

```{r srdevs}
srdevs <- FLCore::rlnormar1(
  n      = it,
  sdlog  = spars["sigmaR"],
  rho    = spars["rho"],
  years  = seq(dy, fy)
)
```

```{r flom_build}
om <- FLom(
  stock      = stki,
  refpts     = refpts,
  sr         = sra,
  projection = mseCtrl(method = fwd.om),
  deviances  = srdevs
)
om
```

```{r fwd_window}
om <- fwdWindow(om, end = fy)
```

```{r oem_perfect}
oem <- FLoem(
  observations = list(stk = stock(om)),
  method       = perfect.oem
)
```

## Short-cut deviations

```{r shortcut_devs}
sdevs <- shortcut_devs(
  om,
  Fcv   = spars["Fcv"],
  Fphi  = 0.5,
  SSBcv = spars["SSBcv"]
)
```

```{r iem}
iem <- FLiem(
  method = noise.iem,
  args   = list(noise = rlnorm(it, rec(om) %=% 0, 0.1))
)
```

```{r save_flom, eval=FALSE}
save(om, oem, sdevs, iem, file = "rdata/flom.sbr.rda")
```

```{r load_flom, echo=FALSE}
load(file = "rdata/flom.sbr.rda")
```

## Setting up harvest control rules

### `shortcut.sa2()` function

```{r shortcut_sa2}
shortcut.sa2 <- function(stk, idx,
                         SSBdevs = unitSums(ssb(stk)) %=% 1,
                         args, tracking, ...) {
  y0  <- args$y0
  dy  <- args$dy
  ay  <- args$ay
  it  <- args$it
  stk <- window(stk, end = dy)
  ind <- FLQuants(
    ssb = unitSums(ssb(stk)) * window(SSBdevs, start = y0, end = dy)
  )
  track(tracking, "conv.est", ac(ay)) <- 1
  list(stk = stk, ind = ind, tracking = tracking)
}
```

### `tac.is2` Function

```{r tac_is2_fun}
tac.is2 <- function(stk, ctrl, args,
                    output   = "catch",
                    recyrs   = "all",
                    Fdevs    = unitMeans(fbar(fut)) %=% 1,
                    dtaclow  = NA, dtacupp = NA,
                    fmin     = 0,  reuse   = TRUE,
                    initac   = metrics(stk, output)[, ac(iy - 1)],
                    tracking) {
  spread(args)
  cys <- seq(ay + management_lag, ay + management_lag + frq - 1)
  fut <- fwdWindow(stk, end = cys[length(cys)], nsq = nsqy)

  if (identical(recyrs, "all")) {
    recyrs <- dimnames(stk)$year
  } else if (is.numeric(recyrs)) {
    all_years <- dimnames(stk)$year
    if (all(recyrs < 0)) {
      recyrs <- tail(all_years, n = abs(recyrs[1]))
    } else {
      recyrs <- all_years[recyrs]
    }
  }

  if (!all(recyrs %in% dimnames(stk)$year)) {
    stop("Specified recruitment years not found in stock object")
  }

  gmnrec <- exp(mean(log(unitSums(rec(stk)[, recyrs])), na.rm = TRUE))
  srr    <- predictModel(model = rec ~ a, params = FLPar(a = c(gmnrec)))
  track(tracking, "gmrec.isys", ay + management_lag) <- gmnrec

  if (isTRUE(reuse) | toupper(reuse) == 'F') {
    ftar <- rep(ctrl[1, ]$value * Fdevs[, ac(cys[1])], length(cys))
  } else {
    ftar <- c(ctrl$value * Fdevs[, ac(cys)])
  }
  track(tracking, "fbar.isys", cys) <- ftar

  if (management_lag > 0) {
    fsq   <- unitMeans(fbar(stk))[, ac(dy)]
    fctrl <- fwdControl(
      list(year  = seq(ay - data_lag + 1, length = management_lag),
           quant = "fbar", value = rep(fsq, management_lag)),
      list(year  = cys, quant = "fbar", value = ftar)
    )
  } else {
    fctrl <- fwdControl(
      list(year = ay + management_lag, quant = "fbar", value = ftar)
    )
  }

  fut <- ffwd(fut, sr = srr, control = fctrl)

  id  <- c(tracking[[1]]["decision.hcr", ac(ay)] > 2) &
         c(unitMeans(fbar(fut))[, ac(ay + management_lag)] > fmin)

  if (isTRUE(reuse) | toupper(reuse) == "C") {
    TAC <- expand(unitSums(catch(fut))[, ac(cys)[1]], year = seq(length(cys)))
  } else {
    TAC <- unitSums(catch(fut))[, ac(cys)]
  }

  prev_tac <- if (ay == iy) rep(initac, length = args$it) else
              c(tracking[[1]]["isys", ac(ay)])

  if (!is.na(dtacupp)) iter(TAC, id) <- pmin(c(iter(TAC, id)), prev_tac[id] * dtacupp)
  if (!is.na(dtaclow)) iter(TAC, id) <- pmax(c(iter(TAC, id)), prev_tac[id] * dtaclow)

  ctrl <- fwdControl(lapply(seq(length(cys)), function(x) {
    list(year = cys[x], quant = output, value = TAC[, x])
  }))
  return(list(ctrl = ctrl, tracking = tracking))
}
```

### HCRs para krill — v2

**Hockey-stick (Fmsy)** — regla tipo ICES como referencia:

```{r hcr_fmsy}
arule <- mpCtrl(list(
  est  = mseCtrl(method = shortcut.sa,
                 args   = list(SSBdevs = sdevs$SSB)),
  hcr  = mseCtrl(method = hockeystick.hcr,
                 args   = list(lim     = 0,
                               trigger = 0.01,
                               target  = Fmsy,
                               min     = 0,
                               metric  = "ssb",
                               output  = "fbar")),
  isys = mseCtrl(method = tac.is,
                 args   = list(recyrs = -2, fmin = 0, Fdevs = sdevs$F))
))
```

**CC145 — TAC fijo CCAMLR actual (145,000 t, subárea 48.1)** (v2):

```{r hcr_cc145}
# v2: HCR de captura constante 145,000 t — propiamente implementada y ejecutada
CC145_hcr <- function(stk, ctrl, args, tracking) {
  niter <- dims(stk)$iter
  tac   <- FLQuant(CFG$tac_cc145,
                   dimnames = list(year = args$ay + args$management_lag,
                                   iter = 1:niter))
  ctrl  <- fwdControl(
    data.frame(year  = args$ay + args$management_lag,
               quant = "catch",
               value = c(tac),
               iter  = 1:niter)
  )
  return(list(ctrl = ctrl, tracking = tracking))
}

arule_cc145 <- mpCtrl(list(
  est  = mseCtrl(method = shortcut.sa, args = list(SSBdevs = sdevs$SSB)),
  hcr  = mseCtrl(method = CC145_hcr),
  isys = mseCtrl(method = tac.is,     args = list(recyrs = -2, fmin = 0,
                                                   Fdevs = sdevs$F))
))
```

**CC620 — TAC fijo propuesto área 48 (620,000 t)** (v2):

```{r hcr_cc620}
# v2: HCR de captura constante 620,000 t — cuota propuesta para área 48
CC620_hcr <- function(stk, ctrl, args, tracking) {
  niter <- dims(stk)$iter
  tac   <- FLQuant(CFG$tac_cc620,
                   dimnames = list(year = args$ay + args$management_lag,
                                   iter = 1:niter))
  ctrl  <- fwdControl(
    data.frame(year  = args$ay + args$management_lag,
               quant = "catch",
               value = c(tac),
               iter  = 1:niter)
  )
  return(list(ctrl = ctrl, tracking = tracking))
}

arule_cc620 <- mpCtrl(list(
  est  = mseCtrl(method = shortcut.sa, args = list(SSBdevs = sdevs$SSB)),
  hcr  = mseCtrl(method = CC620_hcr),
  isys = mseCtrl(method = tac.is,     args = list(recyrs = -2, fmin = 0,
                                                   Fdevs = sdevs$F))
))
```

### Configuración de escenarios Feq

```{r hcrs_grid}
hcrs <- combinations(target = Ftgt, trigger = 0)
hcrs$trigger <- Btri.eq  # Overwrite con Btrigger de krill
```

### MSE arguments

```{r mseargs}
mseargs <- list(
  iy             = dy,
  fy             = fy,
  data_lag       = CFG$data_lag,
  management_lag = CFG$mgmt_lag,
  frq            = CFG$frq
)
cat("MSE: años", dy, "→", fy, "(", CFG$proj_years, "años de proyección)\n")
```

\pagebreak

## Run MSE — todos los escenarios

```{r run_fmsy, warning=FALSE}
# Run 1: hockey-stick Fmsy (referencia)
cat("Corriendo Fmsy.OM...\n")
system.time({
  run_fmsy      <- mp(om, oem = oem, ctrl = arule,
                      args = mseargs, verbose = FALSE)
})
mp.fixexFmsy <- run_fmsy@om@stock
cat("✅ Fmsy.OM completado\n")
```

```{r run_cc145, warning=FALSE}
# v2: Run CC145 propiamente implementado
cat("Corriendo CC145...\n")
system.time({
  run_cc145 <- mp(om, oem = oem, ctrl = arule_cc145,
                  args = mseargs, verbose = FALSE)
})
mp.cc145 <- run_cc145@om@stock
cat("✅ CC145 completado\n")
```

```{r run_cc620, warning=FALSE}
# v2: Run CC620 propiamente implementado
cat("Corriendo CC620...\n")
system.time({
  run_cc620 <- mp(om, oem = oem, ctrl = arule_cc620,
                  args = mseargs, verbose = FALSE)
})
mp.cc620 <- run_cc620@om@stock
cat("✅ CC620 completado\n")
```

```{r run_feq_grid, warning=FALSE}
# v2: Loop Feq limpiado — ahora incluye Fmsy.OM como elemento 1
# En v1 había un bug: el nombre "Fmsy.OM" se asignaba al escenario Feq0.9
# que es el primero del loop — aquí se corrige

ni   <- length(hcrs$target)
runs <- vector("list", ni)

for (i in seq(ni)) {
  hcr_i                  <- arule
  hcr_i$hcr@args$target  <- hcrs$target[i]
  hcr_i$hcr@args$trigger <- hcrs$trigger[i]

  runs[[i]] <- tryCatch(
    mp(om, oem = oem, ctrl = hcr_i, args = mseargs, verbose = FALSE),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("ERROR en escenario", i, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
      NULL
    }
  )
  cat("Feq escenario", i, "/", ni, "completado\n")
}

# v2: FIX — nomenclatura correcta
# En v1: names(runs) = c("Fmsy.OM", paste0(df$Tune[-1], ".Btri.eq"))
# pero runs[[1]] era Feq0.9, no Fmsy — aquí se corrige
nfs       <- length(df$Tune[-1])
tri       <- rep(".Btri.eq", nfs)
scen_feq  <- paste0(df$Tune[-1], tri)
names(runs) <- scen_feq

cat("✅ Grid Feq completado\n")
```

```{r save_runs, eval=FALSE}
save(runs, run_fmsy, run_cc145, run_cc620,
     file = "rdata/mse_runs_v2.rdata")
```

\pagebreak

# RESULTS

## Trayectorias bajo HCRs principales

```{r fig_main_hcrs, fig.height=7, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Trayectorias del stock histórico y proyectado bajo Fmsy, CC145 y CC620"}

# v2: comparar los tres HCRs principales directamente
stk_fmsy <- FLStockR(append(window(om@stock, end = dy), mp.fixexFmsy))
stk_cc145 <- FLStockR(append(window(om@stock, end = dy), mp.cc145))
stk_cc620 <- FLStockR(append(window(om@stock, end = dy), mp.cc620))

stk_fmsy@refpts  <- refpts
stk_cc145@refpts <- refpts
stk_cc620@refpts <- refpts

stk_main <- FLStocks(
  `Fmsy (HCR)`    = stk_fmsy,
  `CC 145kt`      = stk_cc145,
  `CC 620kt`      = stk_cc620
)

thin <- seq(1, it, 5)
plotAdvice(iter(stk_main, thin)) +
  facet_wrap(~qname, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(dy), linetype = 2, col = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(fy), linetype = 2, col = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1970, 3000, 5)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

## Trayectorias Feq-grid

```{r fig9, fig.height=7, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="OM histórico y MSE bajo Fmsy (referencia)"}
stk.fmsy <- FLStocks(
  stock    = window(om@stock, end = dy),
  fixedFmsy = mp.fixexFmsy
)
plot(stk.fmsy) +
  facet_wrap(~qname, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(dy), linetype = 2, col = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(fy), linetype = 2, col = 4)
```

```{r statusfi, fig.height=7, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Stock Status bajo fixed-Fmsy short-cut MSE"}
runR         <- FLStockR(append(window(om@stock, end = dy), mp.fixexFmsy))
runR@refpts  <- refpts

plotAdvice(iter(runR, thin)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(dy), linetype = 2, col = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(fy), linetype = 2, col = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1970, 3000, 5)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

## Visualización de HCRs

```{r hfixed, fig.height=3, fig.width=5, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Advice Rule Current en krill"}
plotGFCM(fadv = Feq, btrigger = Btri.eq, bthr = -1, ftgt = Feq,
         btgt = df$Btgt[2], blim = an(refpts["Blim"]),
         fmin = Feq, bclose = 0, kobe = FALSE, text = FALSE, rel = FALSE,
         xmax = 2.5)
```

```{r hockey, fig.height=3, fig.width=5, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Hockey-stick HCR tipo ICES para krill"}
plotGFCM(fadv = Feq, btrigger = Btri.eq, bthr = -1, ftgt = Feq,
         btgt = df$Btgt[2], blim = an(refpts["Blim"]),
         fmin = 0, bclose = 0, kobe = FALSE, text = FALSE, rel = FALSE,
         xmax = 2.5)
```

```{r alternative, fig.height=8, fig.width=8, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="HCR alternativos con Fmin=0"}
Fadv <- c(df$Ftgt)
Btgt <- c(df$Btgt)
Btri <- c(df$Btrigger)

hcrs_plots <- Map(function(x, y, z) {
  plotGFCM(fadv = x, btrigger = y, ftgt = x, btgt = z,
           blim = an(refpts["Blim"]), fmin = 0, bclose = 0,
           kobe = FALSE, text = FALSE, rel = FALSE)
}, x = Fadv, y = Btri, z = Btgt)

ggarrange(plotlist = hcrs_plots, nrow = 4, ncol = 4)
```

## Resultados grid completo

```{r stks_build}
# v2: FIX — agregar Fmsy.OM explícitamente como primer elemento de stks
# En v1 habia bug de nomenclatura que mezclaba Fmsy.OM con el primer Feq

stks_feq <- FLStocks(lapply(runs[!sapply(runs, is.null)], function(x) {
  out_   <- x@om@stock
  out_   <- FLStockR(out_)
  out_@refpts <- om@refpts
  out_
}))

# Añadir Fmsy.OM y CCs como primeros elementos
stk_fmsy_r        <- FLStockR(mp.fixexFmsy)
stk_fmsy_r@refpts <- om@refpts
stk_cc145_r        <- FLStockR(mp.cc145)
stk_cc145_r@refpts <- om@refpts
stk_cc620_r        <- FLStockR(mp.cc620)
stk_cc620_r@refpts <- om@refpts

stks <- FLStocks(c(
  FLStocks(Fmsy.OM = stk_fmsy_r,
           CC145   = stk_cc145_r,
           CC620   = stk_cc620_r),
  stks_feq
))
```

```{r save_stks, eval=FALSE}
save(stks, file = "rdata/mps.ftune.sbr.rdata")
```

```{r stks_medians}
stkm <- FLStocks(lapply(stks, function(x) stockMedians(x)))
plotAdvice(stkm)
```

```{r compare, warning=FALSE, fig.height=7, fig.width=9, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE,  fig.cap="Comparación de trayectorias bajo HCRs. Paneles: reclutamiento, SSB, F y capturas"}
ref          <- FLStockR(window(stock(om), end = dy))
ref@refpts   <- refpts
pstks        <- FLStocks(c(FLStocks(stock = window(ref, end = dy)), stks))

plotAdvice(iter(pstks, thin)) +
  facet_wrap(~qname, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black", ss3col(length(pstks))[-1])) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("darkgrey", ss3col(length(pstks))[-1])) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(dy), linetype = 2, col = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(fy), linetype = 2, col = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1900, 3000, 5)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

\pagebreak

## Performance Evaluation

Define metrics — incluyendo métricas CCAMLR (v2):

```{r perf_metrics}
metrics <- list(
  SB  = function(x) unitSums(ssb(x)),
  F   = function(x) unitMeans(fbar(x)),
  C   = function(x) unitSums(catch(x)),
  Rec = function(x) unitSums(rec(x))
)
```

```{r perf_stats}
# v2: agregadas métricas CCAMLR (P_075B0 y P_020B0) como estándar CCAMLR
stats <- list(
  a.medianFmsy = list(~yearMedians(F / Fmsy), name = "F/Fmsy",
                      desc = "Median annual F/Fmsy"),
  b.medianBmsy = list(~yearMedians(SB / Bmsy), name = "B/Bmsy",
                      desc = "Median annual B/Bmsy"),
  c.medianCmsy = list(~yearMedians(C / MSY), name = "Catch/MSY",
                      desc = "Median Catch/MSY over years"),
  d.aavC       = list(~yearMedians(iav(C)), name = "AAV",
                      desc = "Median annual variation in catches"),
  e.riskBlim   = list(~apply(iterMeans((SB / Blim) < 1), 1, max),
                      name = "P(B<Blim)", desc = "Probability SSB < Blim"),
  f.P80BMSY    = list(~apply(iterMeans((SB / (Bmsy * 0.8)) > 1), 1, max),
                      name = "B>80Bmsy", desc = "Prob SSB > 80% Bmsy"),
  # v2: métricas CCAMLR específicas
  g.P075B0     = list(~apply(iterMeans((SB / (B0 * 0.75)) > 1), 1, max),
                      name = "P(B>0.75B0)",
                      desc = "CCAMLR: Prob SSB > 75% B0 (escapement)"),
  h.P020B0     = list(~apply(iterMeans((SB / (B0 * 0.20)) > 1), 1, max),
                      name = "P(B>0.20B0)",
                      desc = "CCAMLR: Prob SSB > 20% B0 (collapse limit)")
)
```

## Long-term performance (últimos 10 años)

```{r stoch_msy}
refpts.perf <- refpts
refpts.perf <- rbind(refpts.perf,
  FLPar(MSY = median(unitSums(catch(stks$Fmsy.OM[, ac((fy - 10):fy)]))))
)
cat("MSY estocástico:", round(an(refpts.perf["MSY"])), "t\n")
```

```{r performance_calc, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
perf <- performance(
  stks,
  refpts     = refpts.perf,
  metrics    = metrics,
  statistics = stats,
  years      = list((fy - 10):fy)
)
```

```{r plotlt, fig.height=6, fig.width=7, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Long-term performance metrics — incluyendo métricas CCAMLR (v2)"}
ncol_bp <- length(unique(perf$mp))
plotBPs(perf,
        statistics = c("a.medianFmsy", "b.medianBmsy", "c.medianCmsy",
                       "d.aavC", "e.riskBlim", "f.P80BMSY",
                       "g.P075B0", "h.P020B0"),   # v2: CCAMLR metrics
        size   = 3,
        target = c(a.medianFmsy = 1, b.medianBmsy = 1, c.medianCmsy = 1,
                   g.P075B0    = 0.9),             # v2: target CCAMLR
        limit  = c(e.riskBlim = 0.05, h.P020B0 = 0.99),
        yminmax = c(0.05, 0.95)) +
  theme_bw() +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  ggtitle("Performance: Métricas CCAMLR y estándar") +
  ylab("Performance statistics") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  xlab("Candidates") +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1))
```

## Trade-off plot CCAMLR (v2)

```{r tradeoff_ccamlr, fig.height=5, fig.width=7, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Trade-off CCAMLR: P(B>0.75 B0) vs captura media. Métrica central de evaluación"}

# v2: trade-off específico para CCAMLR con P(B>0.75B0) y P(B>0.20B0)
# Esta figura es la más relevante para presentar en CCAMLR WG-EMM

perf_wide <- perf %>%
  dplyr::filter(statistic %in% c("g.P075B0", "h.P020B0", "c.medianCmsy")) %>%
  dplyr::select(mp, statistic, data) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = statistic, values_from = data,
                     values_fn  = mean)

# Captura media absoluta (desde las trayectorias)
catch_mean <- sapply(names(stks), function(nm) {
  median(an(unitSums(catch(stks[[nm]][, ac((fy - 10):fy)]))))
})
catch_df <- data.frame(mp = names(catch_mean),
                       mean_catch_kt = catch_mean / 1e3)

perf_wide <- merge(perf_wide, catch_df, by = "mp")

# Clasificar tipo de HCR
perf_wide$tipo <- dplyr::case_when(
  perf_wide$mp == "Fmsy.OM" ~ "Fmsy HCR",
  perf_wide$mp == "CC145"   ~ "CC Fijo",
  perf_wide$mp == "CC620"   ~ "CC Fijo",
  TRUE                       ~ "Hockey-stick"
)

ggplot(perf_wide,
       aes(x = mean_catch_kt, y = g.P075B0,
           color = tipo, label = mp)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = h.P020B0), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(nudge_y = 0.02, size = 2.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(145, 620), linetype = "dashed",
             color = c("orange", "red"), alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 7),
                        name  = "P(B>0.20 B0)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Fmsy HCR"    = "steelblue",
                                "CC Fijo"     = "darkorange",
                                "Hockey-stick" = "grey50"),
                     name = "Tipo HCR") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x        = "Captura media últimos 10 años (miles de t)",
       y        = "P(SSB > 0.75 B0)",
       title    = "Trade-off CCAMLR: Escapatoria vs. Rendimiento",
       subtitle = "Líneas: TAC 145kt (naranja) y 620kt (rojo) | Verde: P=0.50") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position   = "right")
```

## Performance Table

```{r perf_table, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

dir.create("perftabs", showWarnings = FALSE)

# v2: FIX — en v1 'statistic' no estaba definido → error silencioso
# Aquí se define explícitamente antes de filtrar
statistic_sel <- c("a.medianFmsy", "b.medianBmsy", "c.medianCmsy",
                   "d.aavC", "e.riskBlim",
                   "g.P075B0", "h.P020B0")  # v2: incluir métricas CCAMLR

pf      <- perf[perf$statistic %in% statistic_sel, ]
df.pf   <- aggregate(data ~ mp + name, pf, mean)
sd.pf   <- aggregate(data ~ mp + name, pf, sd)
sd.pf   <- sd.pf[sd.pf$name == "Catch/MSY", ]
tab.pf  <- cbind(reshape2::dcast(df.pf, mp ~ name), SD.Catch = sd.pf$data)
tab.pf[, -1] <- round(tab.pf[, -1], 3)

write.csv(tab.pf, file = "perftabs/ftune.tab.v2.csv", row.names = FALSE)

kbl(tab.pf, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, align = "lccccc",
    caption = "Métricas de desempeño bajo distintos HCRs (v2: incluye P(B>0.75B0) y P(B>0.20B0))") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"))
```

\pagebreak

# CONCLUSION

This study represents a first implementation of a Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE) for Antarctic krill based on outputs from an integrated stock assessment model. Using a deterministic operating model and closed-loop simulations, we evaluated the performance of current and alternative harvest control rules under uncertainty. The simulations reveal that FMSY-based harvest control rules can maintain the stock near target reference points (B/BMSY ~1.0), though performance is sensitive to recruitment variability and observation errors.

**Version 2 additions**: the analysis now includes (1) a 30-year projection horizon (vs. 10 years in v1), which is necessary to evaluate CCAMLR's long-term escapement objective; (2) explicit evaluation of the fixed TAC rules CC145 and CC620 as proper MSE candidates; (3) CCAMLR-specific performance metrics P(B>0.75 B0) and P(B>0.20 B0); and (4) a trade-off figure comparing all HCRs on the CCAMLR metric space.

Key limitations include: (1) simplified spatial structure, (2) unvalidated stock-recruitment relationships, and (3) preliminary treatment of implementation uncertainty. Future developments should also explore spatial structure and feedbacks from ecosystem indicators to enhance realism and support ecosystem-based fisheries management.

\pagebreak

# CODE REPOSITORY

Krill MSE Repository Overview GitHub Link: [https://github.com/MauroMardones/Krill_MSE/tree/main](https://github.com/MauroMardones/Krill_MSE/tree/main)

```{r session_info}
sessionInfo()
```

\pagebreak

# REFERENCES
